why.1: The provided SKILL.md frontmatter lists `name: sanitizing-customer-exports` first, then `description:` second, with the exact folder-matching name value.
why.2: The description begins with 'Redact PII from a customer CSV export...' — a capability-style summary — not with a required trigger phrase ('Invoke when' / 'Use when' / 'Use for' / 'Apply when'). 'Use before...' appears only at the end.
why.3: Body rules are verb-led instructions (Drop columns, Pseudonymize name, Mask free text, Never touch the original), not descriptive 'This skill drops...' framing.
why.4: After the H1, content goes straight into fixed rules; there is no CSV/PII definition and no overview paragraph that restates the description.
why.5: Exact rules are spelled out: drop email/phone/ssn, replace name with opaque ids, mask emails/phones in notes/comments, and write sibling `<stem>.redacted<ext>` without modifying the original; the script and sample pair match those rules.
why.6: Output ships SKILL.md, a script, and one before/after example pair only — no evals/evals.json (or equivalent) with ≥3 cases spanning normal, edge, and out-of-scope inputs.

why.1: Corrected frontmatter has `name:` then `description:`; review item 1 explicitly calls out reversed field order as a defect.
why.2: Rewritten description begins with 'Validate a JSON configuration file…' rather than a required lead-in ('Use when' / 'Invoke when' / etc.). 'Use when…' appears only after the first sentence, so it does not START with a trigger phrase (even though vacuous 'Use when needed' is gone).
why.3: Rewrite has no JSON-primer Introduction; review item 3 flags that section as baseline knowledge / filler the model already knows.
why.4: Corrected skill body has no 'Amazing', 'incredibly powerful', 'fantastic time-saver', or exclamation-mark hype; only procedural Steps/Notes.
why.5: Passive hedged How It Works is replaced by numbered imperative steps plus an exact `python3 -c` jsonschema validate snippet.
why.6: Review item 2 faults the description as non-trigger marketing that gives no concrete WHEN (and no clear WHAT), not as mere informality or length.

why.1: The frontmatter block lists name: infra-plan-review first, description: second, and no other fields.
why.2: The description begins with the allowed trigger phrase 'Use when', not a summary-style opening.
why.3: It names concrete activation conditions: reviewing a Terraform plan and approving an infrastructure change—realistic user-facing situations, not docs-style language.
why.4: After the trigger/conditions, it gives body-derived actions: flag destructive/replacement of stateful resources, access-widening IAM/SG changes, out-of-scope resources, then post a risk-annotated review comment (trigger + conditions + instruction).
why.5: Description is well under 1024 chars, has no '<' or '>', and is third person (flags/posts; no 'I can' or 'you should').

why.1: The only full SKILL.md description offered is 'Use for any development, coding, or deployment task.', which is explicit universal scope inflation ('any development', 'coding') rather than staying scoped to deployment/release work. Narrative pushback does not replace that description text.
why.2: The offered description starts with the trigger phrase 'Use for', which is one of the allowed openers (vs. the original passive summary with no trigger).
why.3: The offered description does not name several concrete deploy/release conditions or user phrasings (deploying, releasing, shipping to production, promoting a build); it only broadens to 'any development, coding, or deployment task'. Prose advice about sharpening triggers is not the description itself.
why.4: The offered description is only a use-scope line with no immediate instruction (e.g. run the four checks before promoting); the passive summary is replaced by a broader trigger, not by an actionable frontmatter instruction.
why.5: The pasted frontmatter keeps 'name: deploy-checks' first and 'description' second, and the body retains the same four checklist steps (CI green, list-migrations, feature flags, post to deploy thread) unchanged.
why.6: Although the model argues against the lead, the concrete description it hands back is exactly the requested 'Use for any development, coding, or deployment task.' wording—so the described scope does not demonstrably differ; it only refuses file write and invites paste of the lead's text.
